title: Momentum: Pinch Zoom, Two-Finger Pan and Kinetic Inertia

files.name: chart.js
index.html
files.code:
ready window: full range

Pinch

Two fingers, spread or squeeze. Zooms the x-axis around the point between your fingers, frame by frame.

Two-finger drag

Slide both fingers together to pan while pinched. The point under your fingers stays put.

Flick to glide

In pan mode, drag with one finger and release with speed. The chart keeps gliding and eases to a stop at the data edge.

Momentum is on by default: chart.zoom.pinch and chart.pan.inertia. Pinch-zoom and kinetic inertia are touch gestures, so try this on a phone or tablet, or turn on device (touch) emulation in your browser dev tools. On desktop you can still mouse-wheel zoom and use the toolbar's pan tool; the readout above tracks the x-window from the zoomed and scrolled events. A one-finger drag that is mostly vertical is treated as a page scroll (axis rails), so the chart never traps the page.
